name: Chainstack Managed Blockchain Node API
what_it_does: Managed blockchain node infrastructure platform with REST API for provisioning, managing, and accessing blockchain nodes across 25+ protocols — providing dedicated RPC endpoints, subgraph hosting, and multi-network management via API.
use_cases: Provisioning dedicated Ethereum, Solana, or other blockchain nodes via API
Managing blockchain node configurations and network access programmatically
Accessing reliable RPC endpoints with dedicated throughput for production dApps
Hosting The Graph subgraphs on managed infrastructure via Chainstack platform
Monitoring node health and performance metrics for blockchain infrastructure agents
not_for: Development-only usage without production scaling needs (use free Infura/Ankr)
Single-chain usage where Alchemy or QuickNode have better developer tooling
Blockchain analytics without node access requirements
best_when: An agent manages multi-protocol blockchain infrastructure, needing dedicated nodes across 25+ networks with operational API control.
avoid_when: You need developer tooling (debug APIs, trace endpoints) — Alchemy or QuickNode are better focused.

auth.notes: Bearer token for platform management API. Dedicated node endpoints have their own auth (Basic auth or API key per node). Organization-scoped management tokens.

pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $49/month; Business from $399/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Request-based pricing. Dedicated nodes provide guaranteed throughput. Archive node access on paid plans. Competitive with QuickNode for multi-protocol node management.

performance.rate_limits: Requests per month and concurrent connections per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Platform management REST API documented. Node RPC endpoints follow standard JSON-RPC. Error responses include failure reasons. Node provisioning is async — poll for status.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Node reads and RPC queries are idempotent. Node provisioning creates new node each call — check existence first. Subgraph deployment idempotent by name.
known_agent_gotchas: Node provisioning takes minutes — agents must poll for RUNNING status
Each node has unique RPC endpoint URL — dynamic URL management required
Protocol-specific configurations vary significantly across supported chains
Archive nodes required for historical queries — not included on all plans

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. Bearer token for management. TLS enforced. Node endpoints have per-node auth. Multi-region data residency options. Dedicated nodes provide isolation.
